programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Hines completes his final duties as he nears the end of his seven-year posting as rector of the largest Anglican parish in the southern hemisphere, while on Pebble Island Arina and partner Raymond prepare to vacate the farm that their families have had connections with for more than 100 years, destined for a new life in Stanley. programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len Goodman continues to expand his palate in Birmingham, where he has his first taste of Polish food and samples some Thai fish curry. His companion Ainsley Harriott heads to the kitchen to cook a dish inspired by the day's travels(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: In Teesdale, equine specialist Sybil attempts a delicate operation to save a horse's sight, and farm vet Erika is called out to an emergency on a farm where a cow is having difficulty giving birth. In Fife, the team is on tenterhooks to see if a first-time mum will accept her minute-old puppies. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: The importance of power in the animal kingdom, revealing how dominant creatures have the best access to food and water, and are also the most attractive to the opposite sex. An orphaned young chimp tries to climb the social ladder, but his troop is ruled by big, aggressive males and his first attempt to join them ends in a beating. A meerkat society will only allow newcomers in if they know how to deal with a venomous snake. And the only way to occupy the top spot in a meadow of kangaroos is to beat the ruling male in a boxing match - but few have challenged the current incumbent, who stands at 8ft. Will any animal dare take him on? David Attenborough narrates(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Roo Irvine and James Braxton visit the Midlands, where Roo takes a shine to some carriage lamps, while James meets his namesake and haggles over a box of fossils(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Stars of the small screen Jeremy Edwards, Chizzy Akudolu, Susie Amy, Mark Little and Hayley Tamaddon take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Stephen Kember is given a long-mute music box that once belonged to a professional stage magician and toy experts Julia Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch get their hands on a much-loved push-along horse that has seen better days. Other items up for repair are a water-damaged penny arcade game and an old leather-backed chair(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Lynn and Sandra in the Highlands get a surprise when they harvest their first batch of honey. The Rodgers family gather to give bull Mario a good send-off, while Raymond and Johnny discover their sheep are under threat from flystrike, and the only way to protect them is to put the entire flock of 1,600 through the sheepdip(n)

programme.title: Broke
programme.desc: Further real-life stories of people who are in work but struggling to make ends meet. Intensive-care nurse Lorraine has not had a pay rise in eight years and with inflation and the rising cost of living, she earns thousands of pounds less after tax than in 2010. Kevin from North Ayrshire works more than 100 hours a week managing four businesses, yet he still delivers takeaways at night to save for his son's long-term future, while Eryl in Liverpool tops up her zero-hour wage by foraging for foliage and making wreaths to sell(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer return in this one-off version of the 1990 show that originally introduced them to TV audiences - Vic Reeves Big Night Out. The duo promise new characters as well as a few old favourites, surprise cameos and a healthy dose of their trademark slapstick humour. Plus, a very popular television programme receives the R&M makeover treatment. Matt Lucas features among the jamboree, while Mel Gibson and Idris Elba are seen as never before(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Conjoined Twins: An Impossible Decision
programme.desc: Conjoined twins Marieme and Ndeye have separate hearts and lungs, but Marieme's heart is very weak and her life expectancy is poor and if she dies, her stronger sister will die with her. As the girls' health deteriorates, their father Ibrahima and staff at Great Ormond Street Hospital are forced to decide whether a separation should be considered. This programme tracks the journey of Ibrahima and the medical team as they wrestle with is a seemingly impossible decision with the help of the hospital's Clinical Ethics Committee(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Proton beam therapy is the one of the most technologically advanced though expensive cancer treatments in the world - but it has the potential to save the lives of children with otherwise incurable forms of the disease. This programme follows the engineers, scientists and medics as they race to build two new centres, one at the Christie Hospital in Manchester and one at University College Hospital in London, as well as the first children awaiting the lifesaving treatment(n)

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. In the second edition, police officers in Sunderland respond to the fatal stabbing of 62-year-old grandmother Joan Hoggett, and receive a call about a teenager covered in blood. Meanwhile, detectives work tirelessly to investigate a complicated crime spree, where offenders have stolen a pub jukebox, tip jar and charity box and while attempting to flee police have stolen a car and mugged a passer-by(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team focus their attention on the loss of wildflower habitats in the UK and the part that gardeners can play in averting the resulting loss of wildlife. In Lincolnshire, Adam Frost finds out how crucial meadows are to supporting the ecosystem, Arit Anderson visits East Sussex to discover the results of a recent wildlife survey carried out in the gardens there and Nick Bailey travels to Staffordshire where meadows are being grown in gardens for the benefit of wildlife. Plus, Carol Klein identifies plants that can help increase insect and bird populations(n)

programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman visit east London, where the former Strictly Come Dancing judge returns to his childhood sampling some Jewish delicacies and has his first taste of Vietnamese food. programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate Britain's love of seafood and the plentiful and diverse supply of produce obtained from local waters. They also explore the history of Billingsgate Market in east London, the country's largest inland fish market(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Megan helps to rehome 80 caged hens with local families in Fife, and when one is discovered with a broken leg, she decides to take care of it. In Teesdale, farm vet Richard takes drastic action to save the life of a calf with bloat, while Helen meets a Jack Russell puppy that has bitten its own tongue. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how the competition to breed creates scenes of the most extraordinary beauty, as well as some of the most violent battles seen in nature. A male flame bowerbird shows off its artistic prowess to impress a potential mate - but if that does not prove enough, it will demonstrate its dancing skills instead. Waved albatrosses pair up for life and spend hours in each other's company, although things are not so simple for a male peacock jumping spider - one wrong move in its dazzling courtship routine could prove fatal. However, one of the most magnificent displays of all is created by a tiny male pufferfish, which impresses the ladies by building a spectacular underwater 'crop circle' in the sand - arguably the most perfect and complex structure created by any animal(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Roo Irvine head back up the north-west coast on the hunt for precious artefacts. En route Roo heads far out into Morecambe Bay to look for a local delicacy and James runs away to Blackpool to join the circus. Roo finds an object with equine interest, but will it make enough cash at the York auction for her to pip James to the greater profit(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Sophie Faldo, David Domoney, Frances Tophill, James Tanner and Will Kirk form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: University Challenge icons Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull take a road trip with a difference, searching Scotland, England, Northern Ireland and Wales for hidden gems of British scientific and technological ingenuity. They begin with a trip to Blackpool Pleasure Beach to experience Sir Hiram Maxim's 1904 ‘Captive Flying Machines', followed by a visit to Southport, home to the only lawnmower museum of its kind in the world(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys presents the first heat of the latest series of the classic quiz, where contenders sit upon the famous black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to ultimately become the show's latest champion. The specialist subjects in this opening edition are the Lake District, the novels of Sue Townsend, horror writer Stephen King and celebrated chemist Dorothy Hodgkin(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer brave the wild lakes of Essex in search of the mighty carp. They have the latest high-tech equipment to catch the biggest fish they can - and are fully prepared to stay all night if they have to. The pair discuss the true meaning of a mid-life crisis and decide to visit a beauty clinic to see whether there are any suitable treatments available to re-capture their youth. Bob reveals his secret recipe to make the perfect scrambled eggs - but it's not the only thing that shocks Paul(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty advises on essential summer pruning and adds French beans to the vegetable garden. The programme also meets a gardener in Hertfordshire who combines her love of cottage garden plants with tropical plants and visits a garden in Kent where pots are the stars of the summer show(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The early 1970s finds Bob focused on editing the musical movie Cabaret. Gwen, meanwhile, auditions for the leading role in a straight play that forces her to reflect on the beginning of her career, her disastrous first marriage to James Henaghan and a career change that led to her breakthrough performance in Can-Can. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star in this biographical drama detailing the lives of choreographer-director Bob Fosse and celebrated Broadway dancer Gwen Verdon. With Norbert Leo Butz and Peter Scolari(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Joe Lycett, Phil Wang and Ellie Taylor join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig looks at the pastimes of past times. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Sarah Smith(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Edinburgh Nights with Nish Kumar
programme.desc: Nish goes behind the scenes with some of the big names and hopeful first timers in comedy at the largest arts festival in the world. He samples the Los Angeles Philharmonic Orchestra playing for free in a football stadium, marvels at trail biking legend Danny MacAskill creating an extraordinary circus show and tries his hand at beatboxing(n)

programme.title: A Most Violent Year
programme.desc: New York City during the winter of 1981 is a dangerous place to be, but one immigrant is determined to expand his business and support his family. Unfortunately, the unrelenting rise in violent crime threatens to ruin his livelihood and destroy everything he has worked tirelessly for. Thriller, starring Oscar Isaac, Jessica Chastain and David Oyelowo(n)

credits.actor: Oscar Isaac, Jessica Chastain, David Oyelowo

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Stacey Meets the IS Brides - Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley travels to holding camps in Kurdish-controlled northern Syria, where she meets Western women who left their home countries to join Islamic State, and also finds the camps filled with European children. The Kurds in control of this part of Syria say the Western powers should not expect them to be jailers on their behalf indefinitely, and Stacey explores the question of what to do with these women and children who have been left behind(n)

programme.title: Extinction Rebellion: Last Chance to Save the World?
programme.desc: Reporter Ben Zand follows climate-change protest group Extinction Rebellion for four months, as they build toward 'The Rebellion' - 11 days of protest in April - with the aim of taking over and occupying various locations in London. The film also focuses on three young people who have been inspired to join and lead the protests, and Ben speaks to the leader of the movement Roger Hallam, who has spent years academically researching tactics for social change. Ben challenges him on his methods and asks whether it is justifiable to encourage young people to break the law(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Featuring Norfolk's World Championship Thumb Wrestling(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: The team has made it to the final but it feels like nobody has faith in them(n)

programme.title: Deadly Top 10
programme.desc: Steve Backshall chooses his favourite creatures that live in extreme environments, including the snow leopard and a deep-diving whale(n)

programme.title: Penguins on a Plane: Great Animal Moves
programme.desc: Despite the ratings-grabbing title, this two-parter isn't quite the action thriller it sounds. Rather, it's an insightful documentary revealing how thousands of exotic animals are moved around the world every day, and how they cope with being transported. First up, a flock of gentoo penguins get a luxury £40,000 container for their trip from New Zealand to Birmingham, while staff at the West Midlands Safari Park prepare to bid farewell to Pinky the two-ton hippo, who puts her trip to the south of France in danger by testing a reinforced crate to its limits. Plus, a beekeeper awaits a precious cargo of some eight million bees, which he hopes will help British fruit farmers(n)

programme.title: Cats v Dogs: Which Is Best?
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Liz Bonnin present the second of two programmes in which pooches and pussies are put through a number of tests to see which family pet comes out on top. There are five more rounds, concentrating on which animal is easiest to train, communication, which is best at helping reduce people's stress, independence and the big question - do cats and dogs really love their owners? On the search for answers, dog-lover Chris meets a Border Collie which can recognise more than 150 words and Liz meets a cat capable of choosing its own TV channels(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Lynn and Sandra in the Highlands get a surprise when they harvest their first batch of honey. The Rodgers family gather to give bull Mario a good send-off, while Raymond and Johnny discover their sheep are under threat from flystrike, and the only way to protect them is to put the entire flock of 1,600 through the sheepdip(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Bryony Crutcher, 26, admits to passing off shop-bought food as her own creations, but now she's been asked to host her best friend's hen party and needs Lorraine Pascale's help to come up with an impressive meal. The food writer puts together an inspiring canape menu for Bryony to master, including Thai larb gai, bite-sized chocolate Napoleons and delicate coconut meringues filled with ginger cream(n)

programme.title: Secret Britain
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Adam Henson explore the moors of North Yorkshire. Adam meets a farmer whose father uncovered a Roman villa while ploughing, attempts to make a clay pot using 2,000-year-old technology and discovers the remains of a forgotten glass-making industry. Ellie finds out why Britain's highest pub, the Tan Hill Inn, was built miles away from the nearest customers, investigates the Whitby Yards - former slums that have become highly sought as havens of peace - and visits the ruins of Rievaulx Abbey(n)

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part two of two. The secrets behind animals' group games, from dolphins that play catch to a hyena tug of war, revealing the benefits they gain from their playful behaviour. The programme examines how breaching the rules of a game can have fatal consequences - but how it also presents an opportunity for animals with a rigid hierarchy to break down social barriers(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the second and penultimate day of the biennial event, held this year at Zdzislaw Krzyszkowiak Stadium in Bydgoszcz, Poland. Events taking place today include the men's and women's 100m and 400m finals, as well as the men's 1500m and women's 3,000m, with Great Britain and holders Germany among the 12 teams involved(n)

programme.title: Proms Encore
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by composer Hannah Kendall and trombonist Peter Moore as Proms Encore celebrates the next generation of classical musicians. Rachel Parris discovers what makes the new stars tick, Tom Service takes a musical trip with a maestro and soprano Fatma Said gives a special performance on the bandstand(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.sub-title: The Two and a Half Feathers
programme.desc: Jones's glorious military reputation is called into question when new platoon member George Clarke makes scandalous allegations about his conduct at the Battle of Omdurman in Sudan. Comedy first aired in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn and John Le Mesurier(n)

credits.actor: Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn, John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they learn the basic skills needed to face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In California, a three-month old sea otter pup has to learn what's safe to eat in a world enmeshed with humans, while in Sri Lanka, a seven-month old macaque is forced by his mother to toughen up. In Kenya, a five-month old elephant bond with new families(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part two of two. Experts analyse the Princess's marriage to Lord Snowdon at the start of the 1960s. At home with celebrities and artists, they're riding the wave of a cultural and sexual revolution that's transforming Britain. The mystique of monarchy is being increasingly undermined by the emergence of a more open and egalitarian society(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Frankie Boyle, Jack Carroll, Holly Walsh
programme.desc: Glaswegian comedian Frankie Boyle introduces young stand-up sensation Jack Carroll, who rose to fame on Britain's Got Talent in 2013. Holly Walsh will also be taking to the stage at London's Hammersmith Apollo(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Paper Boi is quick to regret his decision to play a show at a college campus that Earn arranged. Comedy drama about a rapper and his manager(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: A flashback to Earn and Al's adolescence, where the authenticity of Earn's shirt is embarrassingly called into question at school(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn runs around town trying to finish everything before catching a plane so that his rapper cousin Alfred, aka Paper Boi, can go on a European tour with Clark County. Comedy drama, starring Donald Glover, about an aspiring music manager trying to turn his life around by making his rapper cousin a star(n)

programme.title: Clouds of Sils Maria
programme.desc: A famous actress prepares to star in a new production of the play that made her career in a supporting part, with the lead role now played by a scandalous Hollywood star. She and her assistant head to a cottage in the Swiss mountains to prepare, which leads her to reflect on the harsh truths about how her life has changed. Drama, starring Juliette Binoche, Kristen Stewart and Chloe Grace Moretz(n)

credits.actor: Juliette Binoche, Kristen Stewart, Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition from April 24, featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, Brenda Russell, the Primitives, Luther Vandross, Joyce Sims, Bananarama, Will Downing, S'Express and Scott Fitzgerald(n)

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: Air traffic controllers guide a passenger jet with a major technical fault safely to the ground, before responding to an unidentified flying object detected on radar screens. Meanwhile, British Airways Captain Paul Bonhomme takes part in an air race at Ascot, and a YouTube blogger realises he must undertake special training for his new camera-drone. Plus, a woman tries to overcome her fear of flying, and a man sets out to prove that he can learn to fly, despite having restricted growth(n)

programme.title: Operation Wild
programme.desc: Broadcaster Clare Balding and vet Steve Leonard travel the world to watch vets perform innovative life-saving operations on a variety of wild animals. This first edition features a baby panda called Zhu Xia in south-west China in need of an MRI scan, and rescued gorilla Shufai in Cameroon, who was shot by poachers when he was a baby. Plus, a manta ray on the Japanese island of Okinawa is given an ultra-sound scan to determine if it's pregnant, and a four-ton elephant in Laos with a gunshot wound(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Helen Czerski explores the science of temperature. She begins by venturing to the bottom of the scale, revealing how cold has shaped the world and why frozen does not necessarily mean what people might think(n)

programme.title: Joan of Arc: God's Warrior
programme.desc: During the Middle Ages, a 17-year-old girl came from nowhere and fronted an army in a world that believed that women could neither fight nor lead. In this film, Helen Castor reveals the reasons Joan of Arc was able to do what she did in a tale of military and religious events mixed with psychological courtroom drama(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of My Family
programme.desc: The documentary charting social history over the centuries tells the story of an Edwardian family locked in benefit dependency, and of a mother who was accused of a shocking crime against her own child. Susan Nelson and her family live on government welfare - did her descendants follow a similar path or did they manage to move up in life? As the film reveals, more than 100 years on, some of them still rely on benefits, while others have freed their families from the grinding poverty of their ancestor(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition from April 24, featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, Brenda Russell, the Primitives, Luther Vandross, Joyce Sims, Bananarama, Will Downing, S'Express and Scott Fitzgerald(n)

programme.title: Immortal Egypt with Joann Fletcher
programme.desc: Archaeologist and historian Joann Fletcher explores the story of Ancient Egypt and begins with a search of the building blocks that made he civilisation so enduring. She discovers how its people changed from primitive farmers to pyramid builders in just a few centuries, and finds early evidence for Egypt's gods and its obsession with death and the afterlife. She also reveals how the first writing was used to calculate taxes and examines Egypt's first pyramid - one of the world's earliest stone structures(n)

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.sub-title: Secret Knowledge: In Search of Rory McEwen
programme.desc: Jools Holland tells the story of his father-in-law, Rory McEwen, the aristocrat, artist, folk singer and pioneering TV presenter widely credited with introducing blues music to Britain. The Hootenanny star brings his relative's story to life with McEwen's paintings, archive material, and contributions from Van Morrison, David Dimbleby and Jonathan Miller(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Helen Czerski explores the science of temperature. She begins by venturing to the bottom of the scale, revealing how cold has shaped the world and why frozen does not necessarily mean what people might think(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Adrian John introduce the edition first aired on May 5, featuring performances by Joyce Sims, New Order, Magnum, Harry Enfield, the Adventures, Narada, Prince, the Christians, the Primitives, S'Express and Star Turn on 45 Pints(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: John Wilson's virtuoso orchestra celebrates the music of one of the most famous film studios, Warner Brothers. The lush sounds of composers such as Max Steiner and Erich Korngold are among the many pieces played showcasing why this historic studio holds a special place in the hearts of lovers of film music. Katie Derham presents from the Royal Albert Hall(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.sub-title: BBC Proms: The Sound of Space
programme.desc: At the Royal Albert Hall, Cerys Matthews introduces a Prom featuring music from sci-fi films. The London Contemporary Orchestra performs excerpts from cult soundtracks together with recent works from Hans Zimmer and Mica Levi. Fans of Alien: Covenant, Interstellar, Under the Skin, or Gravity will recognise this repertoire(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Adrian John introduce the edition first aired on May 5, featuring performances by Joyce Sims, New Order, Magnum, Harry Enfield, the Adventures, Narada, Prince, the Christians, the Primitives, S'Express and Star Turn on 45 Pints(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Guns for Hire: The Story of the Sideman
programme.desc: Earl Slick, David Bowie's sideman of 40 years, looks at the men and women behind some of the greatest songs of the 20th and 21st century. The sidemen are often the forgotten 'guns for hire' that changed musical history and this documentary reveals what it feels like to live in the shadow of a star, a story of triumph and thwarted ambition. Contributors include Mick Jagger, Keith Richards and Billy Joel, as well as sidemen past and present for Prince, David Bowie, John Lennon, Otis Redding, the Rolling Stones and Beyonce(n)

programme.title: Reggae Fever: David Rodigan
programme.desc: The life of the German-born reggae broadcaster and radio presenter whose love affair with Jamaican music began in the 1950s, celebrating a man whose career is strangely intertwined with not only the evolution of such music in this country, but also the evolution of the culture. In recent years, the 67-year-old's DJ appearances have started attracting a far younger audience, a reflection perhaps of the way different forms of music from the different cultures that have arrived in Britain over the last 70 years have integrated, taken root and spawned new scenes, attitudes and tastes(n)

programme.title: The Last Pirates: Britain's Rebel DJs
programme.desc: Rodney P's career as a rapper would not have been possible without the lifeblood of pirate radio airplay in the 1980s. Here, he presents an alternative history of Britain during a time of entrepreneurialism and social upheaval, with archive and music that celebrates a very different side of Thatcher's Britain. His story details how land-based pirate radio broadcasters created a platform for black music, changing the soundtrack of Britain for ever. Featuring contributions by DJs, station owners and Department of Trade and Industry enforcers(n)

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: Scientists and wildlife film-makers led by Steve Backshall, George McGavin and Gordon Buchanan explore Mount Bosavi, a giant extinct volcano covered by thick and largely unexplored rainforest in New Guinea. With the help of trackers from a remote tribe, they search for rare creatures and species to find evidence that may help stop the area's trees being felled for timber. The nest of the world's smallest parrot, an unusual beetle and new types of frog, gecko and bat are among the team's first discoveries(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: June's kidnappers threaten to kill the ferry's passengers unless she gives herself up, while the authorities, alerted by a silent alarm, prepare a plan of action. In Danish(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: Yusuf warns Danish intelligence that if any attempt is made to rescue the ferry hostages they will all be killed. Back on shore, SP and Simon are visited by Mahdi's wife. In Danish(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: Ten years in the making, this series tells the epic story of the Vietnam War, bringing the war and the chaotic epoch it encompassed viscerally to life. In this opening part, Vietnamese revolutionaries led by Ho Chi Minh end nearly a century of French colonial occupation and, with the Cold War intensifying, Vietnam is divided in two at Geneva(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: When Lily is upset to realise Matt is going out with cool girl Greta Masters, Jas plans a make-over slumber party to cheer her up. Unfortunately it clashes with a rare comet sighting, which Martha and Ollie are desperate to experience. They have even been allowed to borrow the very precious school telescope for the evening. Ollie is smuggled in to Lily's house by Martha to watch secretly from the attic, with Martha planning to split her time between both events. After much chaos and confusion, and with the presence of Ollie in the house rumbled, a compromise is finally reached, where the girls all get to do both the make-over and watch the rare comet. At the end of the make-over session Martha and Jas are horrified to see Lily has transformed herself to look exactly like Greta in a bid to impress Matt. To spare Lily's feelings they pretend to like her new look, but are relieved the next day in school to see Lily returned to her normal, natural self. Lily has realised it's better to be(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Martha is eagerly anticipating the visit of the Careers Guidance Counsellor, Mrs Lazarus. It's the perfect opportunity to help her finally decide upon a future career. programme.title: So Awkward

Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

In this edition, he catches up with Wales rugby coach Robin McBryde who is the ceremonial keeper of the sword in the Gorsedd of the Bards, as well as renowned harpist Catrin Finch who is being inducted into this ancient order. Jason also discovers more about the iconic Welsh wrestler Orig Williams 10 years after his death as the Eisteddfod remembers a local legend. Plus, a visit to Maes B, the fringe youth festival, where the up-and-coming bands play alongside established acts(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick goes running but it is clear he is in pain - and things only get worse later when he collapses while referring the kids' football match. Iqra is suspicious of Habiba's sudden illness and catches her sister with Adam. Rainie tries to persuade Bobby to look after her, and Chantelle helps Whitney with her clothing empire, but is unhappy to see her images on social media(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Republic of Ireland midfielder Kevin Kilbane, jockey Sam Twiston-Davies, Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh and Women's Rugby World Cup-winner Maggie Alphonsi join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: After the disastrous Open Day at his house, Phil is nowhere to be found. As Edith and Sandra hurry to Phil's house, Edith receives a phone call from her 'difficult' sister Joan inviting herself to stay. Sandra pulls faces, but Edith feels obliged to agree. The duo enter Phil's house where they find a piano-playing Queenie, but no Phil. Roger visits the recovering Bob in hospital and tells him that he has thought of a new way to make money and he has let his mother's spare room, online, to two Americans. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentine's Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Betty's sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. This time he's on a quest to discover all that's 'weird and wonderful'(n)

programme.title: Hell On Wheels
programme.desc: Roller derby is one of the fastest growing sports in Europe. This documentary reveals the lives and loves of Wales' first and best roller derby team Tiger Bay Brawlers, following them through the biggest moment of their sporting lives - the European Continental Championships. To the uninitiated, roller derby looks like hell on wheels - a violent spinning vortex of tattooed women on roller skates, crashing into each other at high speed. To its devotees, however, it is more than just a sport(n)

programme.title: Room 101 - Extra Storage
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel show presented by Frank Skinner, with David Mitchell, Judy Murray and Anita Rani competing to have their pet hates consigned to Room 101, with topics including sniffing, walking and sugar lumps(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team looks into the death of a terrorist 30 years previously after his daughter receives an anonymous note claiming he was murdered - an investigation that takes them right back to the Greenham Common anti-nuclear protests. Meanwhile, Sasha agrees to go for dinner with her ex-husband Ned, which he is hoping will lead to a reconciliation. Tamzin Outhwaite and Dennis Waterman star, with guest appearances from Barnaby Kay, Charlotte Cornwell and Patricia Potter(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. The walls start to close in on Connie when the hospital board notices the high rate of benzodiazepine prescriptions in the emergency department, and she is forced to choose between her addiction and her career. Meanwhile, Iain takes a stand as he defends the paramedics in the emergency department, and Duffy learns that she does not need to be a nurse to be able to help others(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the opening round of Premier League fixtures, including West Ham United v Manchester City and Tottenham Hotspur v Aston Villa. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick feels the pressure to sort out the Vic's finances, and is later distracted by Tina in her hour of need. Is he taking on too much? The police arrive in Walford looking for their suspect in Phil's attack, Billy struggles to keep his jealousy of Adam in check and Sheree has a suggestion to solve Denise's problems at the salon(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

Fact-based drama, starring Adeel Akhtar and Kiran Sonia Sawar(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In a first for the series, two related celebrities join forces to investigate their family tree as comedian and actor Jack Whitehall and his father and occasional double-act partner and talk show co-host Michael team up to look into their past. They learn about a tragedy that left Michael's grandfather an orphan, and also follow their lineage back to Wales in the 1830s, where they find out about their Tory ancestor and his role fighting against the Chartist political and social reform movement(n)

programme.desc: Mick feels the pressure to sort out the Vic's finances, and is later distracted by Tina in her hour of need. Is he taking on too much? The police arrive in Walford looking for their suspect in Phil's attack, Billy struggles to keep his jealousy of Adam in check and Sheree has a suggestion to solve Denise's problems at the salon(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.title: Murdered by My Father
programme.desc: A widowed father attempts to ensure his daughter is set up with the right husband. However, as her planned engagement approaches, she falls for a charismatic charmer and becomes caught in a conflict between two generations with differing values. Fact-based drama, starring Adeel Akhtar and Kiran Sonia Sawar(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In a first for the series, two related celebrities join forces to investigate their family tree as comedian and actor Jack Whitehall and his father and occasional double-act partner and talk show co-host Michael team up to look into their past. They learn about a tragedy that left Michael's grandfather an orphan, and also follow their lineage back to Wales in the 1830s, where they find out about their Tory ancestor and his role fighting against the Chartist political and social reform movement(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers with a pressing deadline of their own race to refurbish a terraced house in Tylorstown in south Wales, a businesswoman buys a former takeaway to help her business, and a developer got more than he bargains for when he buys a piece of land on the Isle of Sheppey. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as the auction buys are developed(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the local Bloom group in Croston, Lancashire, who are determined to win a gold award despite coping with the aftermath of a devastating flood. With only six weeks until judging day, they have come up an ambitious grand plan to impress the judges, but rallying a community that has been through a difficult time is a huge ask(n)

Elsewhere, Shay is led astray by Siouxsie, and Big Mac launches a plan to entice more tourists to Weld. Erik Thomson stars(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley goes house searching in rural North Yorkshire with a couple who, on their £800,000 budget, want to return to their Yorkshire roots. Their grand plan is to combine country life with their love of cats by running a cattery business. Along the way, Ginny pauses in Harrogate to take afternoon tea and enjoys a nostalgic taste of the region's traditional sweet treats(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: The area surrounding the wasteland upon which the market sits is being developed into attractive and desirable flats. Dagenham is being gentrified and that's given market boss Frank an idea - can they draw in a new, well-heeled sort of customer by going upmarket? Narrated by Larry Lamb(n)

As ever, the two design teams go head to head to create the winning design, with the garden owner having to pick the one they like the most. Who will the couple pick to design their playtime paradise and - most importantly - will their children Ted and Betsy give the winning proposal the all important thumbs-up?(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: After the disastrous Open Day at his house, Phil is nowhere to be found. As Edith and Sandra hurry to Phil's house, Edith receives a phone call from her 'difficult' sister Joan inviting herself to stay. Sandra pulls faces, but Edith feels obliged to agree. The duo enter Phil's house where they find a piano-playing Queenie, but no Phil. Roger visits the recovering Bob in hospital and tells him that he has thought of a new way to make money and he has let his mother's spare room, online, to two Americans. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentine's Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Betty's sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: Police, Camera, Auction
programme.desc: An insight into the auctions where criminals' assets from their nefarious activities are sold at auction, with the proceeds usually going to help those whose lives have been blighted by crime. From sports cars and designer goods to outrageous bling and strange collectibles, cameras reveal the broad range of criminals' possessions seized by specialist police teams and put under the hammer. The auctioneers and police discuss their work, while those victims who will benefit from the money wait anxiously to see the outcome of the sales(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow the stories of three families who have faced challenges building their new relationships. Brenda Rhensius's daughter Joanne has struggled with the new situation because of the loyalty she feels towards her adoptive mother, while Spencer Brown and his sister Margot have been gradually trying to bring their two worlds together. The third reunion was not originally shown and it is only three years that Hazel Stubbs Races and her son Kenneth feel ready to share their experience.(n)

programme.title: John Bishop's Ireland
programme.desc: The comedian travels to Portrush, one of Ireland's most northerly points, where battered by big swells and cold arctic winds, he goes swimming with a local swimming club. In Belfast, John discovers a unique taxi tradition in the Falls Road district, while in South Armagh he meets farmer Jas McDonald for an idiosyncratic take on the reality of living on the border. John performs his comedy routine along the way, from a small and cramped gig in Belfast through to the biggest stage in all of Ireland. He completes his journey in Dublin, capital of the Republic(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott explores Trinidad, where he visits a Hindu temple built on land reclaimed from the sea, prepares grilled tamarind tofu with callaloo pesto, and samples a local speciality - curried chickpeas in a fried bread roll. On a visit to Maracas Bay, he makes a coconut Caribbean chai(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Good Health
programme.desc: Sian Williams and Ranj Singh present a summer travel special, putting together a family health kit for every emergency for just £26, and investigating different brands of suncream on the market to see which provides the best protection and value for money. programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin enjoys one of Britain's National Trails as she walks part of the Thames Path through Oxfordshire - meeting some of the people and animals who call the banks of this impressive river home. Ben Fogle is in Whitby on the North Yorkshire coast to follow one of the nation's favourite dishes - fish and chips - from sea to plate. The programme also catches up with Dales shepherdess Amanda Owen as her family celebrates dad Clive's birthday(n)

programme.title: Liar Liar
programme.desc: A lawyer, whose underhand ploys to win cases leave him little time to see his son, discovers he is incapable of lying after the boy's birthday wish makes his dad tell only the truth. programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott explores Trinidad, where he visits a Hindu temple built on land reclaimed from the sea, prepares grilled tamarind tofu with callaloo pesto, and samples a local speciality - curried chickpeas in a fried bread roll. On a visit to Maracas Bay, he makes a coconut Caribbean chai(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Good Health
programme.desc: Sian Williams and Ranj Singh present a summer travel special, putting together a family health kit for every emergency for just £26, and investigating different brands of suncream on the market to see which provides the best protection and value for money. programme.title: Countrywise

Ben Fogle is in Whitby on the North Yorkshire coast to follow one of the nation's favourite dishes - fish and chips - from sea to plate. The programme also catches up with Dales shepherdess Amanda Owen as her family celebrates dad Clive's birthday(n)

programme.title: Liar Liar

However, this causes a host of problems as the attorney tries to muddle his way through a high-profile divorce settlement. Comedy, with Jim Carrey, Maura Tierney, Justin Cooper, Jennifer Tilly and Cary Elwes(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Robert and his family make their annual summer visit to his Scottish cousins at Duneagle Castle, leaving most of their servants at home. Edith discovers newspaper editor Michael Gregson is also on holiday in the area and may not be there just for the sketching and fishing, while personal servant O'Brien finds a kindred spirit below stairs in the form of her opposite number. programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A case from 13 years earlier returns to haunt Lewis when the evidence comes under review. Graham Lawrie was incarcerated in a secure psychiatric hospital after being found guilty of the murders of three police officers, but now the findings of the forensics lab have been called into question and an appeal has been launched. As Lawrie remains locked up ahead of his day in court, PC Mark Travis is lured to an isolated location with a hoax call and falls victim to a killer who uses the same weapon as that used in the original murders(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. programme.desc: A disgraced English gentleman joins the Foreign Legion to restore his honour, and proves himself an unlikely hero. Comedy, starring Phil Silvers, Jim Dale, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Peter Butterworth, Bernard Bresslaw and Joan Sims(n)

programme.title: Cilla
programme.desc: Reunited with Bobby, Cilla's confidence returns and she once again dazzles crowds with her vocal talents. The re-emergence of impresario Brian Epstein then propels her career to greater heights, but this is quickly followed by a new low when her first record fails to set the charts alight, despite being written by two local lads called John Lennon and Paul McCartney. Epstein suggests a change of direction, persuading the singer to take a gamble on the ballad Anyone Who Had a Heart, against her future husband's advice. Continuing Jeff Pope's three-part biopic charting Cilla Black's rise to fame in 1960s Liverpool, starring Sheridan Smith, with Aneurin Barnard and Ed Stoppard(n)

credits.actor: Sheridan Smith, Aneurin Barnard, Ed Stoppard

programme.title: Wild at Heart

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Blue Train
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the disappearance of a Dutch au pair, which seems connected to rivalry amid the city's academics. programme.title: River Monsters

Armed with a venomous 10in barb on the end of its whip-like tail, this 700lb predator tests the extreme angler to his physical limits, eventually snapping his rod and ripping his bicep(n)

programme.title: British Touring Cars

programme.desc: The bull shark can tolerate fresh water, allowing it to travel far up rivers and into close contact with humans. programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani presents coverage of the Shergar Cup from Ascot, where teams of jockeys competed over six races at 1.05, 1.40, 2.15, 2.50, 3.25 and 4.00 to decide who would be crowned champions. Plus, races from Haydock Park at 2.30 and 3.05, and Newmarket at 2.45. With analysis from Pat Smullen and Kevin Blake, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Sally Ann Grassick, and commentary from Mark Johnson(n)

programme.title: The Horse Soldiers

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris explores five countries that were part of Yugoslavia, which had an extensive state-run railway network before its break-up in the 1990s and the Balkan Wars of Independence that followed. From Slovenia, Chris heads east along an old Austro-Hungarian railway that was once used by the Orient Express, pays a visit to a very unusual railway-related battlefield in Bosnia and rides on one of the world's great mountain railways on his way to the Serbian capital Belgrade before finishing his journey in Montenegro(n)

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell

programme.title: Vegas 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary meeting some of the characters and hearing the stories that make the gambling capital of Las Vegas one of the most fun-filled cities on Earth. Meanwhile, theatre impresario David Saxe searches for new talent for his long-running production Vegas! The Show, while over at a pool party, the staff are topless and spending thousands of dollars on girls and champagne is just a normal day(n)

programme.title: Portillo: The Trouble with the Tories
programme.desc: Part two of two. The former cabinet minister's assessment of the Conservative Party concentrates on Brexit, and how the party has brought itself to the brink of political destruction over arguably the biggest political crisis in Britain since the Second World War. With contributions from Boris Johnson, William Hague, Michael Gove, Michael Heseltine, Norman Lamont and Tony Blair, Portillo considers how splits in the party over widened since the referendum, and how Parliament struggled to deliver on the British people's vote to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.sub-title: When Luxury Holidays Go Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: When holidays go right they offer much-needed rest and relaxation in the lap of luxury. However, those same vacations can just as easily end in disaster. Examples include chartered accountant Bill and his wife Tanya, who paid £11,000 on a once-in-a-lifetime cruise to the Bahamas, only to end up trapped in the eye of a cyclone. Iain and Judie's luxury dream trip to Vegas turned into deadly nightmare when they were caught up in the now infamous shootings in October 2017 in which 58 people were killed. Contributors include Danni Menzies, Scarlette Douglas and Simon Calder. Narrated by Glynis Barber(n)

programme.title: Gotham

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow charts the life of Greek artist Domenicos Theotocopolous, dubbed El Greco, one of the most distinctive painters of the 16th century. His work was considered to be ahead of its time - so much so that it was all but ignored for 300 years. El Greco developed a highly individualistic style that eventually proved enormously influential, paving the way for the Baroque and providing the inspiration for 20th-century expressionism(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels through the Middle East to explore what remains of the colonial railways, which were built there more than 100 years ago. In the Wadi Rum valley, Chris boards a steam train along one of the few bits of the Hejaz railway that remains in working use, following the route through Jordan, before crossing the border into Israel as he heads for Jerusalem, which is sacred to more than half of the world's population. Along the way, Chris visits the ancient city of Petra, as well as Haifa and Tel Aviva(n)

programme.title: Crossrail: Where Did It All Go Wrong?
programme.desc: Crossrail is Europe's biggest engineering project, a new train line linking east and west London that was meant to open in December last year, but it is years behind schedule, billions over budget and dogged by all kinds of problems. This documentary tracks the troubled project from the very beginning, featuring interviews with former workers, politicians and transport experts, as well as people who were promised that Crossrail would bring huge benefits(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging
programme.desc: In Warwickshire, Michael Buerk, Shaun Williamson, Amanda Barrie, Anita Harris and John Prescott settle into new boats, then head out to conquer the Grand Union Canal. First, however, there is the small question of the 21-lock flight at Hatton as the five meet some of the volunteers who restore the locks and keep then functioning. Amanda takes a detour to Warwick Castle in search of her ancestors, while Sean and John, discover the unique joys of sheep-racing. Deep into Shakespeare country, Michael tries to find the famous local pies he remembers from his youth(n)

programme.desc: Tim Marlow explores the life and work of 17th-century artist Peter Paul Rubens, who enjoyed an active and eventful career. He was regarded as the chief exponent of the Baroque style, merging the grace of the Italian High Renaissance with the realism and landscapes of the northern tradition, and was perhaps best known for painting voluptuous female nudes within large-scale mythological allegories(n)

programme.desc: Examining the devastation caused in Carlisle when severe flooding in 2015 left most of the city underwater just before Christmas. Flood victims Stu and Jo reveal their disbelief when their family is left homeless, and aside from dealing with the aftermath of the deluge, they have to contend with the threat of opportunistic looters. Cameras also cover a disastrous basement conversion in a north London flat, a botched boiler installation in Manchester, and a Shropshire-based rental nightmare(n)

David Lean's Oscar-winning Second World War drama, starring Alec Guinness, William Holden and Jack Hawkins(n)

programme.title: D-Day's Sunken Secrets
programme.desc: Using two mini-submarines, a group of historians, wreck detectives, scientists and D-Day veterans go on an expedition to search the seabed for items from the Normandy landings of June 1944. Among the objects the team finds are a collection of American Sherman tanks that were specially adapted to float, and the wreck of the SS Leopoldville, a troopship sunk by a U-boat in December 1944 with the loss of more than 800 lives. Richard Armitage narrates(n)

programme.title: WW2: The True Cost of War
programme.sub-title: The War of the World/Lightning War
programme.desc: Documentary using recorded figures and statistics to outline the full extent of the conflict, explaining the horrors of war and how it ever came to take place. The programme begins by looking at the story of Franz Honiok, a 43-year-old farmer who is often considered the first victim of the Second World War, before going on to show that when Germany invaded Poland in August 1939, no-one was ready for war(n)

programme.title: Schindler's List
programme.desc: In German-occupied Poland during the Second World War, businessman and Nazi Party member Oskar Schindler is deeply affected when he witnesses the clearing of the Krakow Ghetto. He decides to try to save Jews destined for the concentration camps by employing them in his factory - an act of mercy made possible by a deal struck with a sadistic German commandant. Steven Spielberg's Oscar-winning drama, starring Liam Neeson, Ben Kingsley and Ralph Fiennes(n)

credits.actor: Liam Neeson, Ben Kingsley, Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: The experts are in Manchester, where house prices have been known to rise faster than anywhere else in the UK, including London. Kirstie Allsopp is with Katherine and Andrew, who are looking for a property they can renovate into their dream home. They have £185,000 to spend - but money isn't Kirstie's biggest challenge this week - she's facing competition from developers and needs the couple to decide quickly when they see the place they want. Phil Spencer scours the southern suburbs for Sarah and Claude, who have their hearts set on desirable Didsbury. They desperately need more space as Claude's one-bedroom bachelor pad is bursting at the seams, but with their sights set on one of the most expensive areas in the city, Phil has to open their eyes to what their £250,000 budget can buy them in other locations(n)

On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the hidden lives of the animals of Chester Zoo, one of Britain's most popular zoos, using micro-rig technology to capture the behaviour of the animals and their close relationships with their keepers. In the first episode, the zoo's Asian elephant family welcome a new arrival. Grumpy matriarch Thi has been pregnant for almost two years and is reaching the end of her tether. She's given birth to a stillborn baby in the past, so the keepers have a close eye on her. Cameras also follow Red panda Jung, who has been getting the cold shoulder from his partner Nima since they had two cubs(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas returns to narrate another light-hearted retrospective poking fun at Britain's viewing habits in the 1970s. This time, he focuses on the world of comedy, unfettered as it was to the conventions of political correctness. A selection of clips from hit TV shows illustrate how, if viewers were not straight, white, middle-class men, they were at risk of being offended, and highlights examples from On the Buses, The Two Ronnies, Dave Allen at Large and The Wheeltappers and Shunters Social Club. Includes commentary and contributions by Melvyn Hayes, Eric Chappell, Bill Oddie, Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Grace Dent, Nish Kumar, Ellie Taylor, Joel Dommett and Phil Wang(n)

programme.title: Steve Backshall's Extreme River Challenge

He begins his journey inland at Lake Habbema, and braves some of the world's fiercest white water rapids. Along the way, he stays in a village belonging to the Dani tribe and ventures into some previously unexplored caves(n)

programme.title: Walter Tull - Forgotten Hero
programme.desc: Former EastEnders actor Nick Bailey investigates the story of Walter Tull, the pioneering black British footballer who died heroically fighting in World War One. He examines how Walter became the first black officer in the British Army when regulations required men of pure European descent, asks why he was denied the honour of a Military Cross for heroism and explores how he overcame racial abuse(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The archaeologists get an opportunity to dig at an army firing range in High Ham, Somerset, to investigate a set of Roman mosaics first discovered 150 years ago. Initial searches suggest the remains of Roman villas could be located on the site, and the team begins to build up a picture of the highs and lows of life in the society of the time - until the cold weather threatens to bring the excavation to a premature halt. Presented by Tony Robinson(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson gets to choose a site for investigation and decides to take the team to Jersey, where a German anti-aircraft battery was built during the Second World War. As the experts explore the area, they realise it contained a large and complex settlement, and discover shocking facts about the islanders' suffering during the war(n)

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The changing landscape of the coastline from Newcastle to Hull. Mark Horton discovers how laser technology could predict coastal erosion, Dick Strawbridge finds out how Middlesbrough's Transporter Bridge put the city on the map, and Miranda Krestovnikoff heads to Bempton Cliffs(n)

programme.title: Wild Caribbean

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how recent archaeological discoveries have shed new light on culture, origins and capabilities of the very first native Britons. For centuries, it has been assumed Britain's first peoples lives a brutal, hand-to-mouth existence as hunter-gatherers, but fresh evidence has emerged that has forced scientists and anthropologist to reassess their impressions of who these people were. The result is a very different portrait, illustrating a hardy and sophisticated culture that withstood centuries of extreme climate change, including a devastating tsunami, and narrator Adrian Lester describes how these early Britons endured the primal forces and became the first people to populate the island nation that would eventually become known as Britain. Includes contributions by a variety of experts, including Dr Hans Peeters, Dr Jim Innes, Professor Vince Gaffney, Dr David Jacques and Professor Martin Bell(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past
programme.desc: Explorer Hiram Bingham's quest to find a settlement deep inside the Amazon jungle said to have been built by the great Inca Empire after its members fled from the ferocious onslaught of Spanish Conquistadors in the 16th century(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team travel to Derwentcote in Co Durham, where they explore the Industrial Revolution's impact on the region by digging through the undergrowth to search for old furnaces and forges. Their investigation is also helped by the diary of an 18th-century industrial spy, and records from the time help shed light on how the area's cottage industries gave way to the power of expanding cities such as Sheffield(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team search for Roman artefacts beneath the graveyard of St Kyneburgha's Church in Castor, Cambridgeshire. With several reports of similar finds in and around the village, speculation mounts that they could uncover the remnants of a praetorium - a huge building that would have dominated the region's skyline during the period. However, as the experts start digging in other nearby locations, they discover that not all the evidence points to the same conclusion(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: Coast

Miranda Krestovnikoff learns the truth behind the classic Ealing comedy Whisky Galore! on Eriskay, and chemist Mike Bullivant tries to recreate a 19th-century experiment to turn peaty soil into oil(n)

programme.title: Attenborough's Living Lights
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the world of bioluminescence, the often spectacular natural light produced by creatures including glow-worms, fireflies and plankton, using specially designed cameras, some fitted to a new generation of deep-sea submersibles and robots to record instances in the very deep ocean. The programme shows a millipede glowing on the forest floor in California, the mesmerising flashing light patterns of the synchronous firefly, the spooky glow of a rare dragon fish and luminous earth worms in France(n)

programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how recent archaeological discoveries have shed new light on culture, origins and capabilities of the very first native Britons. For centuries, it has been assumed Britain's first peoples lives a brutal, hand-to-mouth existence as hunter-gatherers, but fresh evidence has emerged that has forced scientists and anthropologist to reassess their impressions of who these people were. The result is a very different portrait, illustrating a hardy and sophisticated culture that withstood centuries of extreme climate change, including a devastating tsunami, and narrator Adrian Lester describes how these early Britons endured the primal forces and became the first people to populate the island nation that would eventually become known as Britain. Includes contributions by a variety of experts, including Dr Hans Peeters, Dr Jim Innes, Professor Vince Gaffney, Dr David Jacques and Professor Martin Bell(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Sheridan Smith, who went from singing with her parents in the working men's clubs near their North Lincolnshire home to successful stage performances in Little Shop of Horrors and Legally Blonde - not to mention her countless TV appearances - embarks on a journey to find out where her family's musical talents come from. She is soon hot on the trail of her great-great-grandfather Benjamin Doubleday, a world-class banjo player and musical impresario - but she is surprised by his change in fortunes(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Food writer Mary Berry is one of Britain's best-loved TV cooks, finding fame thanks to her judging role on The Great British Bake Off. A self-confessed go-getter, she wants to trace her family tree to know where she gets her energy and drive from. The journey begins in Bath, where Mary grew up, and takes her to Norwich, where both her grandparents lived. Along the way she uncovers ancestors including a bankrupt book-binder, a self-motivated corset-maker and a third who worked in a much more familiar profession(n)

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevenson's Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The nature series looks at animals that live on some of the world's highest mountains. Cameras show gelada baboons screaming to frighten off predators in Ethiopia, demoiselle cranes struggling to migrate over the Himalayas, snow leopards hunting on the peaks of Pakistan and a wild giant panda nursing a baby in a hillside cave. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough focuses on animals living in deserts. Cameras explore the dry Atacama of Chile, where guanacos survive by licking dew off cacti, and capture the rare sight of wild camels eating snow to stay hydrated on Mongolia's Gobi plains. In the rocky Namibian desert, elephants desperately search for food and lions wander the wilderness hunting oryx(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Camera crews follow the activities of wildlife in the Arctic and Antarctic, including an emperor penguin colony's nine-month migration across sea ice to its breeding ground. Clara and her one-year-old daughter Clarita are chosen for release back into the wild, but a flash flood threatens the mission. Meanwhile, the vets provide around-the-clock care for Erik, who has a dangerous infection, and attempt to reintroduce Dilla to her daughter Delilah(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

Two patients who came to the UK from Iran are brought in - one suffering from chest pains, the other having difficulty speaking. They and their friends and family share their memories of the revolution and how it affected their lives(n)

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Helicopter pilot Paul, 59, is embarking on his first-ever date dressed in women's clothing and hopes that 51-year-old Sydney can see that underneath he is 'just a normal bloke', while make-up artist and mother-of-five Bianca, 31, sits down for a meal with white van man and fellow single parent Daniel(n)

programme.title: Britain's Giant Pet Food Factory
programme.desc: A sneak peek inside Mars Petcare's factory in Melton Mowbray, which is operational all day, every day to meet a target of one million packets of pet food a day. There is also a 14-acre research centre where new products are tested. Staff are encouraged to bring in their pets for the happiness of the work environment. But the animals must abide by the company etiquette and today one rebellious dog is in danger of being handed his notice(n)

programme.title: Jade: The Reality Star Who Changed Britain
programme.desc: Ten years on from her death, this series explores the rollercoaster life of Jade Goody, one of the most loved - and hated - reality TV stars, to tell a bigger story of class, politics and cultural change in Britain, at a time when reality TV became the biggest and most polarising form of entertainment in the country. Featuring exclusive interviews and never-before-seen footage, the series explores how Jade's life story mirrors a period of astonishing social change in Britain(n)

programme.title: Born Famous
programme.desc: In an exploration of social mobility in the UK, four people who are the children of celebrities live in their parents' old communities to find out what sort of life they might have had if their mothers or fathers not become hugely successful in their chosen fields. In the first edition, Gordon Ramsay's son Jack heads to the Bretch Hill estate in Banbury, Oxfordshire, to discover what life could have been like had his dad Gordon not become a world-famous chef(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
