programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and the team are in Lancaster to find out how much air pollution a typical family breathes in and whether trees could be the answer to the problem. Chris van Tulleken explores the world of probiotics, Gabriel Weston examines a novel cure for migraines that was discovered by chance in a beauty clinic, and Saleyha Ahsan demonstrates how to revive a victim of drowning(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: The Most Dangerous Man in Tudor England
programme.desc: Melvyn Bragg explores the story of 16th-century scholar and theologian William Tyndale and examines the lasting impact of his mission to translate the Bible into English. The broadcaster reveals how Tyndale's quest made him a profound threat to the authority of both Church and State, setting him on a collision course with emissaries of Henry VIII and the pope(n)

programme.title: Death Drums Along the River
programme.desc: A British inspector is called to Africa to investigate the murder of a fellow policeman and uncovers a diamond-smuggling operation seemingly connected to an upriver hospital. Mystery, based on Edgar Wallace's novel Sanders on the River, starring Richard Todd, Marianne Koch, Albert Lieven and Walter Rilla(n)

programme.title: They Made Me a Fugitive
programme.desc: Soho black marketeers frame a former RAF pilot for murder, forcing him to go on the run until he can exact revenge on them. Thriller, starring Trevor Howard, Sally Gray and Griffith Jones(n)

credits.actor: Trevor Howard, Sally Gray, Griffith Jones

programme.title: London Live Sessions
programme.desc: Fickle Friends give an intimate performance at a London venue(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Horizon: OCD - A Monster in My Mind
programme.desc: Developmental psychologist Uta Frith, a professor of neuroscience at University College London, sets out to dispel misconceptions about Obsessive Compulsive Disorder, which some regard as simply being overly fussy about tidiness, but which is often a much more serious, and potentially crippling brain disorder. She introduces Sophie, who must constantly check she hasn't killed people wherever she goes, and Richard, who is terrified of coming into contact with bins. Also featured is the story of Nanda, who is undergoing pioneering brain surgery to stop her worrying about 'components' on her body. Having explored the impact OCD can have on people's lives, Uta investigates therapy available to those struggling with the condition, and outlines whether neuroscience can offer anything by way of a cure(n)

programme.title: Dukes, Hustlers and the Real Casino Royale
programme.desc: On his death in 2000, John Aspinall was best known for his love of wildlife, but in the 1960s he ran world-famous casino the Clermont Club in London's Mayfair. There he created a gambling world of glamour and excitement, with aristocrats, film stars, MPs and millionaire businessmen arriving nightly. However, it has been claimed that Aspinall's involvement with gangland boss Billy Hill led to cheating at the card tables - although Aspinall's family and friends deny any criminal activity. This film mixes testimony of club members with dramatic reconstructions to tell the story of the alleged con(n)

programme.title: 198 Grand

programme.title: Mulholland Drive
programme.desc: An actress arrives home to find a mysterious woman in her room who is suffering from amnesia. programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary investigating scientific breakthroughs in research on Alzheimer's disease, which are bringing hope to millions of sufferers across the world. New scanning and gene technology is allowing scientists to identify the disease at its earliest stages, often 15 years before symptoms appear and brain cells are destroyed, with drug trials in Colombia, USA and Europe showing startling success. The programme also reveals the changes in lifestyle that can prevent the development of the disease, as well as the effects of a UK-wide trial(n)

programme.title: Make Bradford British
programme.desc: Part one of two. Observational documentary exploring the issue of what it means to be British for Bradford residents who, despite sharing the same nationality, are all from different races and backgrounds. The experiment begins with a cross-section of citizens from the city taking a government test to demonstrate their knowledge of British culture. Eight people who fail the test, including pub landlady Audrey, taxi driver Mohammed and former magistrate Maura, are then placed in pairs and encouraged to swap lifestyles under the supervision of two experts in community relations(n)

programme.title: Long Weekend
programme.desc: A couple take a camping holiday at a remote beach to try and rebuild their struggling relationship. Their disrespect for the wildlife and the environment provokes the wrath of a supernatural presence, and the torment they endure from it brings tensions between them to the surface. Horror, starring John Hargreaves and Briony Behets(n)

programme.title: My Name Is Lenny
programme.desc: Biopic of bare-knuckle fighter Lenny McLean, exploring his troubled upbringing and early days in the unlicensed boxing world, and his career-long rivalry with fellow fighter Roy Shaw. Starring Josh Helman, Michael Bisping and John Hurt in one of his final roles(n)

credits.actor: Josh Helman, Michael Bisping, John Hurt in one of his final roles

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: Nils and Martin save Akka from a great danger. Nils gets all the praise and Martin becomes jealous(n)

programme.title: Mud Men
programme.desc: Steve Brooker and Johnny Vaughan explore the site of the 1917 Silvertown munitions factory explosion, and join the crew of HMS St Albans to sample life in the Navy. They also travel back to the Second World War to experience the Blitz and find out about working on the front line of London's top-secret defences(n)

programme.title: Operation Amsterdam
programme.desc: A stiff-upper-lipped British Army officer engages in a spot of larceny to aid the war effort. Ordered to Amsterdam, he joins forces with two cunning Dutchmen and steals a secret cache of diamonds from a bank vault, thus preventing the gems falling into Nazi hands. Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok(n)

credits.actor: Peter Finch, Tony Britton, Eva Bartok

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary investigating scientific breakthroughs in research on Alzheimer's disease, which are bringing hope to millions of sufferers across the world. New scanning and gene technology is allowing scientists to identify the disease at its earliest stages, often 15 years before symptoms appear and brain cells are destroyed, with drug trials in Colombia, USA and Europe showing startling success. The programme also reveals the changes in lifestyle that can prevent the development of the disease, as well as the effects of a UK-wide trial(n)

programme.title: The Purple Plain
programme.desc: A Canadian squadron leader's increasingly strange behaviour begins to worry the officers of an RAF station deep in the Burmese jungle. There is further concern when the unstable pilot crashes his plane miles from the base, forcing him to make an exhausting trek back to safety. Second World War drama, starring Gregory Peck, Bernard Lee, Brenda de Banzi and Maurice Denham(n)

credits.actor: Gregory Peck, Bernard Lee, Brenda de Banzi, Maurice Denham

programme.title: The Traitors
programme.desc: British and American secret agents join forces to unmask Communist spies plotting to steal top secret plans. Thriller, starring Patrick Allen and Jacqueline Ellis(n)

programme.title: Hip Hop to Opera Holland Park

programme.title: Heyday TV
programme.desc: A retrospective review of Bowie's classic album, featuring extensive interviews with Woody Woodmansey and the late Trevor Bolder(n)

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events showcasing London's vibrant culture scene(n)

programme.title: Mud Men
programme.desc: Steve Brooker and Johnny Vaughan search the banks of Rotherhithe and explore the rich history of shipbuilding and trade on the Thames. They also reveal the origins of the London Underground and get to grips with a 19th-century steam ship(n)

programme.title: Dukes, Hustlers and the Real Casino Royale
programme.desc: On his death in 2000, John Aspinall was best known for his love of wildlife, but in the 1960s he ran world-famous casino the Clermont Club in London's Mayfair. There he created a gambling world of glamour and excitement, with aristocrats, film stars, MPs and millionaire businessmen arriving nightly. However, it has been claimed that Aspinall's involvement with gangland boss Billy Hill led to cheating at the card tables - although Aspinall's family and friends deny any criminal activity. This film mixes testimony of club members with dramatic reconstructions to tell the story of the alleged con(n)

programme.title: The Princess and the Gangster
programme.desc: Exploring the association between Princess Margaret and underworld figure John Bindon, who are portrayed in reconstructions by Nichola Lindsay and Norman Murray. A chance meeting between the pair in Mustique led to rumours of compromising photographs and secret meetings at Kensington Palace(n)

programme.title: Hummingbird
programme.desc: A haunted special forces soldier returns to London from Afghanistan and assumes another man's identity, hoping to make a fresh start. He is drawn into the city's criminal underworld, where his skills prove valuable to the highest bidder, and plots revenge against the gangster who killed his girlfriend. Crime thriller, with Jason Statham and Agata Buzek(n)
